Aizoan, Aizoon canariense, is a succulent plant species native to the Canary Islands, known for its unique, branching growth habit and small, white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been linked to various health benefits, including wound healing, immune system support, and protection against oxidative stress. Aizoan's bioactive constituents include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can be found in traditional Canarian medicine and modern herbal supplements.
